title: Model Library uninstall/install cluster — tier-family shared-dep uninstall + resumable-download corruption + partial-install bar accuracy (LTX-2.3)
description:
UMBRELLA CARD (user wants all related Model-Library dep/download bugs tracked here, not split). Surfaced while user cleared disk space by uninstalling LTX-2.3 High then Balanced and re-installing Balanced. THREE distinct bugs:

=== BUG 1 — Uninstall never frees tier-family shared deps [FIXED, awaiting live verify] ===
Uninstalling a tier-family model (LTX-2.3 High + Balanced share every non-transformer dep: Gemma CLIP, video/audio VAE, text-projection, spatial-upscaler, 3 baked LoRAs = ~19GB) never deletes the shared deps even when NEITHER tier is installed. ROOT: routes/downloadManager.js `_localSharedDepsMap` protected a dep if ANY other model had that file COMPLETE ON DISK (per-dep test, MPI-216, meant to protect mid-install siblings). Circular for a tier family: uninstall High keeps files 'because Balanced has them on disk'; uninstall Balanced keeps them 'because High has them on disk' — each protects the other's copy of the SAME files while both transformers are absent -> undeletable cluster, ~19GB stranded. CONFIRMED IN LOG: `uninstall ltx-23-balanced: removed 1, kept 8 shared` (the ~19GB). FIX SHIPPED (option C): protect a shared dep only when the sibling is whole-model `installed === true` (mirrors the already-correct remote guard `_remoteSharedDepIds`, which gates on entry.installed) OR the dep has a live in-flight job (`_depJobs` refCount>0). Removed the per-dep on-disk test. Remote path already correct (no change needed) — twin-checked per feedback_check_both_engine_paths. Verified: node require loads clean; 4-assertion logic test passes (scratchpad/test_shared_dep_guard.js: old guard strands gemma, new guard frees it when no sibling installed, protects when a sibling IS installed, protects when in-flight). NOT committed yet. TEST PLAN: after user installs Balanced, uninstall it with High absent -> ~19GB shared deps MUST be trashed, bars drop to near-0 (Gemma-for-LTX is LTX-only so it goes). Inverse: with Balanced installed, uninstalling a DIFFERENT model must NOT trash LTX shared deps.

=== BUG 2 — ResumableDownloader corrupts large file across stall/Pause-Resume -> SHA256 mismatch [DIAGNOSED, not fixed] ===
LTX transformer (fp8, 25.2GB) install stalled at ~0 bytes, user Pause->Resumed, it streamed then FAILED verify with SHA256 mismatch (expected 0a1d7aac..702370f0, got c75f0ad2..173559). PROVEN: R2 source is PERFECT — downloaded the 25GB via plain curl (single clean stream, no resume) and sha256 == registry expected (0a1d7aac..). So NOT a hash-registry problem (no /mpic-compute-dep-hashes needed). The app produced corrupt bytes across the stall+Pause/Resume cycle; single-stream curl was fine. SUSPECT (not proven): the resume-offset in ResumableDownloader.download() (routes/downloadManager.js ~532-544, `resumeFromFile({downloaded: partial.downloaded})`) or the reconnect machinery stitches bytes at a wrong offset -> duplicated/gapped range -> corrupt file. The SHA check correctly caught it and scrubbed the file (line 408 fs.remove), which is why the file kept vanishing. Note _maybeRecoverSlowStream is already DISABLED (MPI-129, returns early) due to a prior resume-race that crashed the server. WORKAROUND FOR USER: fresh single-stream download, do NOT pause -> verifies clean. NEEDS: reproduce + fix the resume-offset corruption; this is the serious one (users on flaky connections hit it; only SHA save prevents shipping a corrupt weight).

=== BUG 3 — Progress bar % inaccurate on partial/resume installs [OBSERVED, not fixed] ===
During the Balanced install the library bar read ~89-94% while the transformer (57% of the 44GB pack) was only ~22% downloaded — user correctly spotted the math was off. On a partial/resume install only the MISSING dep is queued, so the live download job denominator = queued-dep(s) only (live /comfy/downloads/active showed ONE dep, the transformer), while the tile's idle _computePartial counts ALL deps on disk -> the two disagree and the % jumps around. custom_nodes are ALREADY excluded from the byte ratio (_byteRatioExcludingNodes, MPI-231, after nodes inflated a bar to '203MB/15MB') — so nodes are NOT the current inflator; the partial-install queued-only denominator is. Reconcile the active-download aggregate and the idle partial so the bar reflects true pack fraction on resume/partial installs. Low severity (cosmetic) vs Bug 2.

SEQUENCE: (1) user installs Balanced single-stream now; (2) restart NOT needed for install; uninstall test needs no restart either (fix already loaded post-restart); (3) verify Bug 1 fix via uninstall; (4) then tackle Bug 2 (downloader) — needs a server-code change + restart; (5) Bug 3 last (cosmetic). Use handoffs to continue in-card as context fills.
